name: Pepper (Capsicum annuum) (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight; representative ripe red sweet pepper (Capsicum annuum)

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Dominant free sugar; USDA measured glucose 1.94 g/100g.

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Co-dominant free sugar; fructose slightly exceeds glucose in ripe red fruit.

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Not detected in USDA panel; ripe Capsicum annuum accumulates hexoses, not sucrose.

components.name: D-galactose
components.evidence: Not detected.

components.name: maltose
components.evidence: Not detected.

components.name: starch
components.evidence: Ripe pepper is essentially starch-free; carbohydrate is free hexoses + fiber.

components.name: dietary fiber
components.evidence: Largely non-fermentable structural polysaccharide.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Song et al. 2023 PMC9871545 (Capsicum annuum fruit quality, HPLC)
components.evidence: Dominant organic acid, 39-64% of total; measured 1.82-5.60 mg/g FW, midpoint ~3.7 mg/g = 0.37 g/100g.

components.name: malic acid
components.evidence: Second major di-acid; 0.99-1.88 mg/g FW, midpoint ~1.4 mg/g = 0.14 g/100g.

components.name: fumaric acid
components.evidence: Reported second most abundant, 1.48-3.71 mg/g FW (18.8-33.8% of total); value is variety-variable and unusually high vs other foods, flag for verification.

components.name: alpha-ketoglutaric acid
components.evidence: 0.70-1.43 mg/g FW (10-17% of total); TCA-cycle intermediate, fermentable.

components.name: oxalic acid
components.evidence: Minor, <8% of total, <0.7 mg/g FW; conservative midpoint used.

components.name: quinic acid
components.source: Wahyuni et al. / chili metabolite HPLC-MS lit (81.3 mg/100g DW pericarp)
components.evidence: Trace on FW basis after DW->FW conversion (~8% dry matter).

components.name: L-ascorbate (vitamin C)
components.evidence: 128 mg/100g; both a vitamin and a reducing organic acid, exceptionally high in red pepper.

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: Low total protein; amino acids below are protein-derived profile.

components.name: L-aspartate
components.evidence: Most abundant amino acid (free + bound); major N source.

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: Second most abundant amino acid.

components.name: L-methionine
components.evidence: Limiting sulfur amino acid.

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: Dominant cation.

components.name: phosphate (P)
components.evidence: Phosphorus 26 mg/100g.

components.name: selenium
components.evidence: Trace but genuine micronutrient.

components.name: vitamin C (ascorbate)
components.evidence: Also listed as organic acid above; defining nutrient of red pepper.

components.name: vitamin A (retinol activity equiv.)
components.evidence: From provitamin-A carotenoids (red pepper).

components.name: biotin (B7)
components.evidence: Present but not quantified in USDA panel; trace.

components.name: total fat
components.evidence: Very low-fat matrix.

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: Dominant fatty acid.

components.name: water
components.evidence: Matrix water content.

remove.name: Silver
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant metal, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Cadmium
remove.reason: toxic heavy-metal contaminant, not a nutrient

remove.name: Lead
remove.reason: toxic heavy-metal contaminant, not a nutrient

remove.name: Mercury
remove.reason: toxic heavy-metal contaminant, not a nutrient

remove.name: Arsenate
remove.reason: toxic metalloid contaminant, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Silicon
remove.reason: non-nutritive ubiquitous trace element, not a defined microbial nutrient

remove.name: Boron
remove.reason: plant micronutrient present in trace amounts; not a defined microbial growth nutrient

remove.name: 17alpha-ethynylestradiol
remove.reason: synthetic pharmaceutical estrogen; biologically implausible in pepper, FooDB predicted/erroneous

remove.name: N-Methylaniline
remove.reason: aromatic amine, implausible non-nutrient/contaminant, FooDB predicted

remove.name: Solanidine
remove.reason: potato steroidal glycoalkaloid; implausible/predicted for Capsicum, not a growth nutrient

missing_added: individual free sugars with amounts (glucose 1.94, fructose 2.26 g/100g; sucrose absent)
keep_uncovered: epsilon-polylysine (genuine phytochemicals/antimicrobials with no BiGG metabolite)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 170108 (Peppers, sweet, red, raw)
Song et al. 2023, Comprehensive fruit quality assessment of green pepper (Capsicum annuum L.), PMC9871545 (organic acids by HPLC)
Wahyuni et al., metabolite profiling of Capsicum (quinic acid pericarp reference)
Frontiers Plant Sci 2025 doi:10.3389/fpls.2025.1719537 (ascorbic acid by ripening stage)
notes: Curated as ripe red sweet Capsicum annuum. Dominant carbon/energy sources are the free hexoses glucose (1.94 g) + fructose (2.26 g, ~230 mM combined) with sucrose absent, backed by citric acid as the dominant organic acid (~0.37 g/100g) plus malic and TCA-cycle acids; ascorbate is exceptionally high (128 mg/100g) and doubles as a reducing organic acid. Organic acids, previously entirely missing, were the main gap filled. Fumaric-acid value is high and variety-dependent (flagged). Removed 13 non-nutritive contaminants/implausible predicted compounds (heavy metals, ethinylestradiol, solanidine, N-methylaniline); genuine capsaicinoids, capsianosides, carotenoids, phenolics and phytosterols retained as uncovered.
